title: Evangelion to other serious mecha
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion to other serious mecha             Quote Post by GAP » Sun Mar 13, 2011 1:01 am I had yet to watch Zambot 3, Daidarn 3(?) or any of Tomino's non gundam series (although I did see the Be Invoked on Yotube) but how does Evangelion compare to those works? Especially since they all use damaged protagonists as pilots and some of them have some psychology references.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Mar 13, 2011 11:03 am Evangelion was almost as good as Dai-Guard. Almost.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sun Mar 13, 2011 11:06 am Mobile Suit Gundam is Evangelion rip-off

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Sun Mar 13, 2011 11:12 am Getter Robo is an Evangelion rip-off

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Mar 13, 2011 2:00 pm Xard wrote: Mobile Suit Gundam is Evangelion rip-off

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sun Mar 13, 2011 2:10 pm InstrumentalityOne wrote: Getter Robo is an Evangelion rip-off

[]: Re: Evangelion to other serious mecha             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Sun Mar 13, 2011 2:39 pm GAP wrote: I had yet to watch Zambot 3, Daidarn 3(?) or any of Tomino's non gundam series (although I did see the Be Invoked on Yotube) but how does Evangelion compare to those works? Especially since they all use damaged protagonists as pilots and some of them have some psychology references.

[]:             Quote Post by Jessemon » Sun Mar 13, 2011 3:05 pm I havn't watched a wholelot of mecha anime (or anime in general tbh), but out of what I HAVE watched, I really liked Mobile Suit Gundam 0080: War in the Pocket a LOT. It's only a six episode OVA, but it was really well done. It's much more character driven than most Gundam animes, and takes a really interesting perspective on war. For example, there is only 1 truly "evil" person in the entire show, and he isn't a central character. It's hard to post much else about it without spoiling anything, but I think it's a pretty darn good show.

[]:             Quote Post by GAP » Sun Mar 13, 2011 10:52 pm I did see Char's Counterattack and it was basically Vietnam in space, but Gundam has to be unrealistic real robot show I had seen so far. Its entertaining but Tomino sure loves his kill 'em all just look at Ideon:Be Invoked, a kid got his head decapitated by a fire extingusher when the Ideon went berserk. Getter Robo is an Evangelion rip-off

[]:             Quote Post by Jessemon » Sun Mar 13, 2011 11:03 pm GAP wrote: I did see Char's Counterattack and it was basically Vietnam in space, but Gundam has to be unrealistic real robot show I had seen so far. Its entertaining but Tomino sure loves his kill 'em all just look at Ideon:Be Invoked, a kid got his head decapitated by a fire extingusher when the Ideon went berserk. Hell any mecha is a ripoff of Evangelion for that matter.

[]:             Quote Post by GAP » Mon Mar 14, 2011 12:18 am I guess the smae can be said about Eva, most of the other Gundams are pretty much about mobile suits.

[]:             Quote Post by Jessemon » Mon Mar 14, 2011 9:32 am GAP wrote: I guess the smae can be said about Eva, most of the other Gundams are pretty much about mobile suits.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Mon Mar 14, 2011 9:11 pm Jessemon wrote: Gundam Wing is actually a huge exception IMO. I think Wing gets a really bad rap in English speaking countries because of it's truly god awful English dub. Wing actually has a pretty complex plot containing a LOT of ideals about what war is and how it should be fought. Most people see GW and think, "Oh yeah, that's that anime that reuses the same battle animations a thousand times and has that one Gundam who kills everything with a giant laser cannon." However, it's MUCH more than that.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Mar 15, 2011 12:14 pm Jessemon wrote: Gundam Wing is actually a huge exception IMO. I think Wing gets a really bad rap in English speaking countries because of it's truly god awful English dub. Wing actually has a pretty complex plot containing a LOT of ideals about what war is and how it should be fought. Most people see GW and think, "Oh yeah, that's that anime that reuses the same battle animations a thousand times and has that one Gundam who kills everything with a giant laser cannon." However, it's MUCH more than that. It's really hard to post a summary because of everything that goes on, but trust me, it's not the grade school hero named Heero who kills everything anime that it's reputation implies.

[]:             Quote Post by Combs » Tue Mar 15, 2011 11:17 pm I've seen it argued multiple times that Eva is and Ideon ripoff. Can anyone who has seen this anime verify any similarities?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Mar 15, 2011 11:29 pm Wiki link
